comment_text.sn36.25:1.1: This is describing the night of the Buddha’s awakening, using the same phrasing found in the first discourse (SN 56.11). | PTS edition does not number this as a separate discourse, hence its numbering is one-off from here to the end of the Saṁyutta.

root_text.sn36.25:1.1: “‘Imā vedanā’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu cakkhuṁ udapādi, ñāṇaṁ udapādi, paññā udapādi, vijjā udapādi, āloko udapādi.
root_text.sn36.25:1.2: ‘Ayaṁ vedanāsamudayo’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu cakkhuṁ udapādi …pe… āloko udapādi.
root_text.sn36.25:1.3: ‘Ayaṁ vedanāsamudayagāminī paṭipadā’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu cakkhuṁ udapādi …pe…
root_text.sn36.25:1.4: ‘ayaṁ vedanānirodho’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu cakkhuṁ udapādi …pe…
root_text.sn36.25:1.5: ‘ayaṁ vedanānirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu cakkhuṁ udapādi …pe…
root_text.sn36.25:1.6: ‘ayaṁ vedanāya assādo’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu …pe…
root_text.sn36.25:1.7: ‘ayaṁ vedanāya ādīnavo’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu …pe…
root_text.sn36.25:1.8: ‘idaṁ kho nissaraṇan’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu cakkhuṁ udapādi, ñāṇaṁ udapādi, paññā udapādi, vijjā udapādi, āloko udapādī”ti.

translation_text.sn36.25:0.2: 3. The Explanation of the Hundred and Eight
translation_text.sn36.25:1.1: “‘These are the feelings.’ Such was the vision, knowledge, wisdom, realization, and light that arose in me regarding teachings not learned before from another.
translation_text.sn36.25:1.3: ‘This is the practice that leads to the origin of feeling.’ …
translation_text.sn36.25:1.5: ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of feeling.’ …
translation_text.sn36.25:1.8: ‘This is the escape from feeling.’ Such was the vision, knowledge, wisdom, realization, and light that arose in me regarding teachings not learned before from another.”
